title: Fragment: The Record
description: Found at the empty guardpost in the deepest chamber of the Whispering Cave after defeating the Warden. A memory crystallized in stone — the Warden's final private thought, preserved in the silence he kept for three hundred years.

lines: The guardpost was empty. The Warden's armor was gone — no trace of it, no fragments, not even an impression in the stone where he had stood for so long.

But the stone itself had changed. A faint warmth ran through the rock here, in the deepest chamber of the Whispering Cave, as if something had been held in place long enough to leave its temperature behind.
In the wall — barely visible in the low light — a shallow impression, like a handprint but not a hand. Something pressed there in a final, deliberate moment.

steps.text: The impression was warm to the touch. And then a voice — not heard exactly, but present, the way a sound is present after it has already finished.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: Thirty-seven parties. I never wrote it down anywhere.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: The record exists only because I counted.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: When I stop counting, the record stops existing.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: I wonder if that's true of everything. I wonder if anything exists except in someone's counting of it.

steps.text: The warmth faded. In the impression in the wall, where the Warden had pressed something at the last moment, a small piece of stone had come loose — a fragment, flat and unremarkable, except that it was warm, and it stayed warm.
It felt, very faintly, like being watched over.

steps.name: Old Guard Tally
steps.description: A fragment of the Warden's memory, crystallized in stone. Warm to the touch. Someone is still counting. Grants +2 to party defense when outnumbered.
